entry.title: SAP Career Journey
entry.content:
I am happy to be a part of this learning group. I am looking for learning materials to start my journey in SAP MM and SAP S/4 Hana Private and Public Cloud for career change management and development. I look forward to getting your feedback on this.

Thank you.

Regards.

Kehinde.

entry.title: Metadata update has been deferred issue in SSAM
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are using MDK Client version 25.6.3 Branding and extension version 2505.1 metadata 2310 and followed below steps to build client

  • copy SAM.mdkproject folder from branding zip downloaded from service market place and place in the MDKclient folder
  • create metadata folder - include custom application in this folder
  • create extensions folder and copy plugins from service market place (2505.1)
  • run install command followed by create-client command - here path is changed from template.mdkproject to SAM.mdkproject  - application is created successfully
  • create build and deploy application in android studio

After application is login, when we click on "Later"   - encountering issue "metadata update is differed . click on retry". Attaching screen snap for reference . However, when we click on Now - application from BTP is loaded successfully in Android emulator. Not sure why application that is build locally getting issues with metadata

Please suggest.

Regards,

Sravanthi

entry.title: Re: How to check connection between sales order item cate...
entry.content:
Perform the below steps:

  • Step 1: Go to SSCUI 102807 Assign Item Categories and review mappings.
  • Step 2: Check product master item category groups for materials in question.
  • Step 3: Review flexible item category determination rules (BRF+) for custom logic.
  • Step 4: Test by creating sales orders with different materials and document types to see which follow‑on documents are generated.

entry.title: Your opportunity to Influence SAP – SAP Customer Engagement Initiative 2026-2 for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud is a complete, modular Cloud ERP software designed for every business need – powered by AI and analytics. With SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you can run your mission-critical operations in real time from anywhere, introduce new business models in your industry, and expand globally with a trusted partner.  

An exciting opportunity to influence SAP has begun with the second cycle in 2026. The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative enables you as an SAP customer or partner, to get early insights into SAP’s product developments and directly work with the developers to define and shape future product directions. 

The projects will be open for customer registration until  June 19, 2026. 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud projects for registration: 

 

ERP Private Cloud:

  • Agentic AI in SAP S/4HANA Procurement (Private Cloud Edition)
  • AI in SAP GRC for SAP HANA

 

ERP Public Cloud:

  • Finance Intelligence on SAP Business Data Cloud
  • Accelerate Integrations with AI powered SAP Integration Suite
  • Modernization Approaches in Purchase Order (SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition)
  • Parallel Unit of Measure
  • Intelligent Returns: Process Discovery & Automation Roadmap
  • Agentic AI Smart Billing Assistant
  • AI-Powered Warranty Verification – Co-Innovate the future of service, repair & claims

 

Seize this opportunity to make your voices heard – register on influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 2 in 2026! If you are also interested in other SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects that are currently open for registration, check out the full list here. 

Warm regards, 

Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Restrict Maintenance Notification conversion to only Reactive Maintenance Orders
entry.content:
We are trying to restrict Maintenance Notification conversion to only Reactive Maintenance Orders in the Manage Maintenance Notifications app.

We identified that the restriction field AUFART (Order Type) is available in business catalog SAP_EAM_BC_MN_MNG_PC (via fields like AUFART_IWERK, AUFART_WERKS, etc.). However, these same restriction fields are also used in catalog SAP_EAM_BC_MP_ORD_MNG_PC, which is responsible for maintenance order creation.

Our concern is that restricting AUFART at the catalog level would remove order type options not only during notification conversion but also in the “Create Maintenance Order” app, leading to unintended cross-app impacts.

Could you please advise on the best approach in S/4HANA Public Cloud to restrict notification-to-order conversion specifically to Reactive Maintenance, without affecting direct order creation or other apps?

entry.title: Sap analytical cloud free trail to more 30 days
entry.content:
Hi All,

I would like to know that if I need to extent days of my Sap analytical cloud free trail to more 30 days then i cant see any option in my tool, it already been nearly to expire now I need for extending, kindly help me and assist how to resolve.

Thanks,

kanchan

entry.title: Re: How to reduce/adjust the excess depreciation posted i...
entry.content:
For Income Tax Act you need to use the dedicated functionality and not rely on the standard depreciation keys to ensure you are compliant. The SAP Help Guide has a comprehensive section in relation to this Act, showing the steps from beginning to end. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/634261119fec4d58970471f2c4a9a740/38c875f128b24a55986d911ea8ff42f8.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

entry.title: Re: SAP Ariba Source to Contract 2605 Release Key Innovations Preview
entry.content: Has feature Large Attachment Support (SOURCE-2797) been released, the article is not available anymore?

entry.title: Re: 3-Level Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer
entry.content: Hi @sumita_nagpal, can you please help us by providing supporting documents like config guide, Enhancements info(if you people have done it) etc.

entry.title: AI in Action: Discover the Latest SAP CX Toolkit Innovations
entry.content:
Are you ready to put AI to work in your SAP Commerce Cloud landscape? Join our live session to explore how the SAP CX AI Toolkit is driving the next generation of intelligent, scalable, and customer-centric commerce experiences.

This concise, technically focused session is designed to help you quickly understand how AI can elevate your digital commerce strategy—with practical insights you can apply immediately.

Why Join This Live Session?

AI is no longer a future concept—it’s actively transforming how commerce operates today. In this session, SAP experts will walk you through the latest innovations in the SAP CX AI Toolkit, showing how you can enhance customer experiences, streamline operations, and stay ahead of the curve.

Whether you're a Developer, Architect, or Commerce Professional, you'll gain actionable knowledge to bring AI into your everyday workflows.

What You’ll Learn

In this focused session, you will explore:

  • Key features of the SAP CX AI Toolkit
  • How AI enhances customer experience and personalization
  • Ways to optimize and automate commerce operations
  • The latest innovations and updates shaping SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Practical use cases you can apply right away

Who Should Attend?

This session is ideal for:

  • SAP Commerce Cloud Developers and Architects
  • Technical Consultants and Product Owners
  • Anyone looking to apply AI in real commerce scenarios

Part of the Commerce AI Live Series

This session is part of our Commerce AI Live Series, designed to showcase real-world AI applications across SAP Commerce Cloud.

Explore more sessions and expand your AI knowledge:
https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-blog-posts-by-sap/elevate-your-commerce-strategy-ai-focused-live-sessions-for-sap-commerce/ba-p/14377931

Register Now

AI in Action: Discover the Latest SAP CX Toolkit Innovations
https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/introducing-the-sap-cx-ai-toolkit-for-sap-commerce-cloud

Stay Ahead with AI-Driven Commerce

AI is redefining digital commerce, and this is your opportunity to get hands-on with the tools shaping that transformation.

Don’t miss this session, learn from experts, gain practical insights, and start building smarter commerce experiences today!

#SAPCommerceCloud #CXAI #DigitalCommerce #AIInnovation #LiveSession #CustomerExperience

entry.title: Change of Delivery Class of existing Database tables from 'A' to 'C' for CUBCO
entry.content:
@patrick_winkler As part of clean core approach we are about to change the delivery class type of existing  database tables from 'A' to 'C' to create Business Configuration Maintenance Object.

These table entries were being maintained by the end user but while creation they chose 'A'.

Will it create any impact if we change delivery class of existing tables.

entry.title: Re: Environment, Health, and Safety in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Hi,

Thanks for outlining the innovations for SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Release in the area of SAP Environment, Health, and Safety.  Is there any integrations with Inspection and Safety Observation whereas, there are high potentials for the reporting of the Inspection based safety Observation (Unsafe Conditions & Unsafe Acts) , so that the business can have the reference of the inspection (inspection ID) as a navigable link to the Safety Observation entry ? If it is not available now, this can be a part of future innovation.

Kind Regards,

Hanish Shan

SAP EH&S Expert - NTT DATA Business Solutions UKI

entry.title: Discovering the Lead to Cash Business Process
entry.content:
Learning Outcome

After participating in this live session, you will be able to:

  • Understand the high-level flow of the Lead-to-Cash lifecycle and key business milestones
  • Describe core SAP CX components and master data involved (customer, product, pricing, contracts)
  • Explain how leads convert to opportunities, quotes, orders, and invoices in SAP
  • Understand typical integrations with CRM, CPQ, order management, billing, and financials

 

Why Attend a Live Session?

If your are beginner and you are willing to have an interactive learning experience, this course is a great fit for you. Join our Lead to Cash course that is designed for Developer, Consultant, Business User and Architect.

Register Today

Register today to start building the skills that will move you forward.

This live session course will take place on 11th of June:

https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/discovering-the-lead-to-cash-business-process?searchId=db7fb666-246c-4710-bf7d-7854925374b4&listPosition=36

entry.title: Post valuation for real estate contract on multiple ledgers
entry.content:
When I post valuation for a real estate contract it only post against one ledger. Either on the one maintained in the valuation rule or determining ledger from accounting principle (if not maintained in rule) How can the system post valuation on all ledgers without assining multiple rules in the contract?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Post valuation for real estate contract on multiple l...
entry.content:
Thank you for your question!

Valuation rules are always accounting principle and ledger dependent. The valuation rules determine how the valuation for the respective accounting principle is calculated and to which ledgers or accounts the postings are made.

If it is required to post valuation against several ledgers for a lease contract, then at least one valuation rule for each required ledger should be maintained on the contract valuation parameters.

This standard system logic, as well as further important considerations related to valuation postings are outlined in the best practice test script available for the CLM scope items (1T6 linked as an example). It is highly recommended to read through these best practice documentations to learn more about the solution and the standard system logic related to lease contracts.

entry.title: Re: 🇩🇪 SAP CodeJam ABAP Cloud (Dortmund, Germany)
entry.content: Hello everyone,  I have RSVP’d for the SAP CodeJam ABAP Cloud event in Dortmund and I am looking forward to attending. See you all there! 🙂

entry.title: Crystal Report Parameters Automatically Mapped as From Date, To Date, Branch, and Cancel in SAP B1
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Is there any setting in SAP Business One to predefine or map Crystal Report parameters automatically?

I have defined the parameters correctly in both the Stored Procedure and Crystal Report, but after deploying the report in SAP B1, the first four parameters are automatically shown as:

  • From Date

  • To Date

  • Branch

  • Cancel

instead of the actual parameter names defined in the report.

Could anyone please confirm if there is any SAP B1 setting, cache, or parameter mapping behavior related to this?

Thank you.

entry.title: How to schedule Monthly Physical Inventory Creation for CC Indicator 'A' only in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am working on an SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud implementation and looking for a way to automate the creation of Physical Inventory Documents specifically for materials marked with Cycle Counting (CC) Indicator "A" (Monthly).

Current Scenario: We have categorized our materials into A, B, and C indicators. We want the system to automatically generate the PI documents for 'A' items on a monthly basis without manually selecting them each time, and business don't want documents getting created automatically for "B" &"C" indicator.

What I’ve Tried:

I am using the Schedule Physical Inventory Document Creation app.

I can see the job template for "Cycle Counting," but I am struggling to isolate the job, so it strictly runs for materials due under Indicator 'A' on a specific monthly frequency.

Any guidance on the configuration or job scheduling setup would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Re: Crystal Report Parameters Automatically Mapped as Fro...
entry.content:
Hello 

There is no SAP Business One setting to automatically map Crystal Report parameters.
This usually happens because SAP B1 is reading cached/old parameter metadata or the report was imported with an old parameter structure.

Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: Sap analytical cloud free trail to more 30 days
entry.content:
Hello @Yash_Singh_Singh 

SAP Analytics Cloud free trial extension is usually not available from the SAC tool itself.
You can try these options:

Check your registered email for any trial extension link from SAP.
Log in to SAP Store / SAP BTP Cockpit and check if extension option is available.
If no option is shown, raise a request with SAP Support / SAP Sales.
Otherwise, create a new trial account using another eligible email ID.

So there is no direct setting inside SAC to extend the free trial manually.

Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: The Universal Documents (Total) button is not registe...
entry.content:
Hi,

SAP are still working with old floppy disks 🙂

To release data you have to press the 'Floppy disk' save icon thing:

Universal Documents (Total) is just a report showing you how it will look after release.

Br

Martin Jul

entry.title: Re: Success Factor :ECP Payroll for Country Kazakhstan
entry.content:
Dear Neeraj,

The ECP local version is not yet delivered in Kazakhstan. However, there is SSF Employee Central and H4S4 Payroll OnPrem in place.

entry.title: Re: WebGUI Short Dump GETWA_NOT_ASSIGNED in CL_HTTP_EXT_I...
entry.content:
Hello @pinnie 


This is most likely user-specific WebGUI/ITS session or personalization corruption.
Not authorization issue. It is likely ITS/WebGUI user-session, personalization, or SAP kernel/ITS bug
Ask Basis team to check, Kill your active sessions in, SM04 / AL08,
Clear HTTP/WebGUI sessions in SMICM

Reset/delete your WebGUI personalization.
Check your user menu/roles for invalid WebGUI entries.
Test by copying your roles to a new user.
If still same, check/apply SAP Notes for.

 

Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: EMail not received from CALM
entry.content:
Hello @shivakul54 


Test email works, the issue is not mail server related. It is most likely due to missing subscription, incorrect notification rule, scope/tag mismatch, or user email configuration.


Verify the user is added as recipient/subscriber for the alert/event.
Check if email notification is enabled in SAP Cloud ALM notification settings.
Confirm the correct event type / managed tag / scope is assigned.
Check if alert conditions are actually triggered.
Verify user email address in IAS / SAP Cloud ALM user profile.
Check spam/junk folder and mail rules.

 

Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: Is that possible to change SAP Cloud ALM alert to oth...
entry.content:
Hello 


In SAP Cloud ALM, alert/email language is generally based on the tenant/user/browser language settings, not directly controlled per alert in Integration & Exception Monitoring.

Change user language in SAP Cloud Identity / user profile.
Change browser language to Japanese (JA).
Log out and log in again to SAP Cloud ALM.
Check if notification/email template supports Japanese.

 


Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: Additional Email Notification to other recipients aft...
entry.content:
Hello @michi_lin76 


In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, there is currently no standard configuration to send Sales Order approval completion emails to additional arbitrary users based on Sales Office.

 

Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: Charts / KPI Visualization with RAP Custom Entities
entry.content:
Hello @Marsh 


There is no fully standard/recommended way to build rich chart/KPI visualizations directly on RAP custom entities. Use CDS analytical views with UI annotations, or build a custom UI extension for custom-entity-based charts.

 

Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: ADT Keyword Highlights Are (STILL) Broken
entry.content:
Hello @cafeteria_fraiche 


This looks like an ADT editor syntax-highlighting bug, not an ABAP syntax issue.


Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: LTMC for recipe with MIC
entry.content: @former_member830911 I hope you are doing well, if you received asnwer from SAP side please update.

entry.title: Re: Metadata update has been deferred issue in SSAM
entry.content:
Hello 


This usually happens when the locally bundled metadata is not detected/loaded correctly, so MDK waits for metadata from the server. When you click Now, it downloads metadata from BTP and works.


Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: Change of Delivery Class of existing Database tables...
entry.content:
Hi @manichand_ale 

When designing the data model of your ABAP application, you should separate system, configuration, and application data. You can then select the correct delivery class for each table. Delivery class A is intended for application data. So if you use it for a table with configuration data, I think the data model will be inaccurate. An inaccurate data model makes it difficult to maintain the application.

In addition, the delivery class also has technical consequences you need to consider for example during client copy or BC Set deployment: https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_latest_index_htm/latest/en-US/index.htm?file=abenddic_database_tab...

When changing the delivery class specifically from A to C you should not have any data loss. The main impact is about the change in behavior described in above link.

Regards,
Patrick

entry.title: Re: Crystal Report Parameters Automatically Mapped as Fro...
entry.content: Can you please let me know how I can fix this so that the correct parameters I defined in the report show up in SAP Business One selection criteria?

entry.title: Re: Anyone else using AI to help build SAP B1 reports?
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm a 20-year-old experienced developer, and I use AI in my daily work. Nothing new — we already had automation before; otherwise, we'd still be scraping bits today.

I've used AI to write SQL queries, but never to build a Crystal Report from scratch until now. Regarding your questions:

  1. Are others using AI for SAP B1 SQL work? What's working, what isn't? - I use to create scripts and to improve existing ones. Like everything AI generated it needs validation after, so I can rewrite some parts of it.
  2. What kinds of reports do you find most painful or time-consuming to build? - The ones with to much sub-details and sub-reports. But that was always like that, even before AI.
  3. How do you handle data-entry inconsistencies that affect report outputs? -  I prefer to handle in the SQL query most of the time. There are some cases when data that needs to be reviewed prior , that can be done by a user or db adjustment routine.

 Best regards,

William Wachholz - https://williamwachholz.vercel.app/

entry.title: Re: Sap analytical cloud free trail to more 30 days
entry.content: Thanks for your prompt response, will try these options.

entry.title: Re: Beyond UAT: Why System Integration Test is the True Backbone of SAP S/4HANA Success
entry.content: @Yogananda Thank you for your feedback, I updated the blog and uploaded a larger version of the image to improve readability, It is now awaiting review before publication.

entry.title: Re: GRIR Account sustitution User exit
entry.content:
Hello @leah_huang71 ,

Your issue occurs because GGB1 + user exit substitution runs too late in the posting process. By that time, SAP has already derived OIM behavior, sort key, BSIS currency fields, and clearing logic from the original GR/IR account (431050). To solve this correctly, the account replacement must happen earlier during MM/FI account determination (before FI document enrichment), using an earlier enhancement point or BAdI rather than FI substitution logic.

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Change Absolute Tax Exchange Rate when exchange rate...
entry.content:
Hello @yuki_0717 ,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, manually changing the document exchange rate does not always automatically recalculate the absolute tax amount using that manual rate, because the SAP tax engine still follows standard tax determination and currency conversion logic. Standard Public Cloud configuration does not generally provide a simple option to freely override the calculated absolute tax amount during posting. If this is a recurring legal/business requirement, the recommended approach is usually to implement a controlled manual tax adjustment process or review the tax code and posting design rather than relying on manual exchange-rate overrides alone.

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Alteração de codigo de imposto em lançamento advindo...
entry.content:
Bom dia,

 

Você deve realizar o procedimento de configurar as regras específicas para a nota de remessa na tela de "Determinação de Código de Imposto." Esse é um procedimento que exige um conhecimento a nível de consultor SAP B1. Posso estar indicando alguém para realizar essa configuração para você. Tenho um formulário de contato no meu site pessoal indicado abaixo.

 

Atenciosamente,

 

William Wachholz - https://williamwachholz.vercel.app/

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One Down Payment Reconciliation Error
entry.content:
Hello @Thanigai_vel ,

I am not an expert on SAP Business one, commenting based on my knowledge. The error occurs because SAP Business One does not allow a regular Incoming Payment to be directly reconciled with a payment originating from a Down Payment Request. The remaining 7,700 INR should not have been posted as a normal Incoming Payment. The correct approach is to reverse/cancel that payment if possible and process the refund through the proper AP Down Payment adjustment, cancellation, or credit memo process so the document flow remains consistent.

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Crystal Reports 8 license doesn't allow subreport manipulation
entry.content:
I'm trying to make modifications to an old VB6 application using Crystal Reports 8. Yes, I know that it's been sunset, but I'm desperate for help. I was given the CDs along with the license information and installed everything on a virtual Windows XP box. Everything works fine until I try to work with a report that has subreports or anything that manipulates the report object using the craxdrt dll. It's saying that an additional license is required. I have no access to said license nor anyway to find it. Is there any way to resurrect this? Does anyone have one? I'm willing to pay? Any advice on how to work this out would be greatly appreciated. 
FYI: The exe works perfectly fine, so it was obviously (to me) built with the proper license information. But, when I build a new exe with the dlls from the CD I was given, then I get the errors.

entry.title: Name 3 field in the Bank data of BP
entry.content:
Hello Team,

Good day.

In bank data screen of BP, few fields (Name, Street, City) of bank details are currently displayed from BNKA table. We are trying to display Name 3 field of Bank also in this screen. When I was working on the bank entity earlier, it was not recommended to modify any attributes in bank entity as it connected across multiple objects. 

Is there any OSS to display Name 3 field in the same UIBB of bank screen in BP instead of creating new UIBB?

Regards,

Raghu

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One Down Payment Reconciliation Error
entry.content: Hello @Divyesh_Mistry, Thank you for your response. Actually, Downpayment was raised a month ago. So, Cancellation is less possible if any other ways to tally that amount.

Can we monitor Host CPU utilization for the DB host in ABAP system just like the application host?

 

Thankyou!

entry.title: Re: Celebrate Your SAP Academic Community Conference experience: Earn Your Exclusive Conference Badg

entry.title: Re: Lemur maps integration with SSAM
entry.content: The map extension is part of the SSAM so when you create any business object in the map, it updates the same entities. For notification, we update MyNotifGeometries entity set with geometry data

entry.title: ISO 4217 currency codes for precious metals XAU, XAG, XPT, and XPD required.
entry.content: We require support regarding ISO 4217 currency codes for precious metals such as XAU, XAG, XPT, and XPD.

entry.title: LIVE SESSIONS on SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps – Data & AI Content Updates
entry.content:
Our next LIVE SESSION, in collaboration with the SAP Global User Group Organization, will take place June 09, 2026, at 17:00 (CET). Join us to hear from Michael and Mirna who will take you through the recent SAP Enterprise Support updates including:

  • SAP Datasphere in Business Data Cloud: CQC Deployment Readiness and CQC Solution Optimization
  • SAP HANA Cloud: Expert-guided Implementation Workshop Series
  • SAP Business AI & Joule: Expert-guided Implementation Workshop Series

 

 

➡️Join live or watch the replay: LIVE SESSIONS on SAP Enterprise Support value maps – Data & AI Content Updates

❓In the live session, you will have the opportunity to ask questions in Q&A. You will find more details in these links:

  • Ensuring a Smooth SAP Datasphere Deployment: The Value of Continuous Quality Checks
    • Continuous Quality Check Info Sheet: CQC-for-Deployment-Readiness_Datasphere.pdf
    • Continuous Quality Check Info Sheet: CQC-for-Solution-Optimization_Datasphere.pdf
  • SAP HANA Cloud: Expert-Guided Implementation Workshop Series
  • New Expert-Guided Implementation: Unlocking Joule and SAP Business AI from Strategy to Activation
  • New Expert-Guided Implementation: Joule Prerequisites and Activation

Note: Access to the Expert-guided Implementation (EGIs) require a one-time registration to SAP Learning

 

SAP Enterprise Support

Providing targeted guidance and self-service content, SAP Enterprise Support is part of the Foundational Success Plan – included with every SAP cloud solution. Content and services include self-paced learning materials, workshop-style interactive remote sessions and continuous quality check (CQC) remote delivered services. Leverage the SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps to get advice on the appropriate SAP Enterprise Support content and services for your needs.

Enhance Your SAP Experience with Value Maps

SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps — your go-to team for Foundational Success Plan guidance and enablement — have a dedicated SAP Community Group. Watch our short demo video, and go to our page to explore our range of topics. To access our Community Group, sign in with your S-user ID or Universal ID. Within the group:

  • Select Data and Analytics Value Map
  • Select SAP Business AI, Clean Core & Sustainability
  • Click “Join Group” and “Subscribe” to stay updated
  • Request a call with an SAP Enterprise Support topic expert for guidance tailored to your goals

 

About the author: 
Caroline Sheridan is a Topic Expert for SAP Enterprise Support value maps, specializing in SAP Data and Analytics.

💡Have questions or feedback on this content? Feel free to leave a comment below or contact @Caroline-S

entry.title: Re: Advanced Returns Management for Supplier Returns
entry.content:
Step 01: Manage Service Order App > Create (use SRVO)

Step 02: Manually input sold to party, Employee responsible , Maintain Item “AIRBUS_SERV-ITEM”; Then go Inhouse section header only and enter the Equipment No and Save.

Step 03: In background, an In House repair number generates, from where we do Receive from Customer (RE2 order is created) - In-Bound delivery is created and when PGR is done 657 movement.

Step 04: In T.code "MSR_INSPWH" Here by Default, we are seeing 0027 and not allowing to save. 

We have used Non valuated as well as Valuated material but same error comes. (This is in 2025 S4 SM version)

If we bypass this error, we see Availability is updated " Received by Service Center" Other wise we keep getting error and the availibility is not updated properly.

 

Need Solution for this.

entry.title: Re: May Developer Challenge - Week 3: Vectorizing the event payload
entry.content:
Thanks @ajmaradiaga for the challenge!

A snippet code:

The vector embedding response:

I used the SentenceTransformers library with the all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embedding model to generate vector representations of Business Partner event data. It runs locally without requiring API keys or external services It is lightweight and fast (suitable for development and prototyping).

entry.title: SAP University Alliances Academic Board established for EMEA region
entry.content:
🚀 A Historic Milestone for Academia–SAP Collaboration Across EMEA
We are proud to announce the official launch of the SAP University Alliances Academic Board – EMEA, established on 1st May 2026.
The new EMEA Board complements other regional Academic Boards worldwide and aims to become a strategic bridge between academia and the SAP ecosystem — connecting research, innovation, talent development, and digitally enabled organisations across one of the most diverse regions in the world.
It represents an extraordinary academic ecosystem with SAP University Alliances partnerships involving: 🏛️ 850+ universities, 🎓 200,000+ students, from 🌐 ~50 countries across Europe, the Middle East and Africa.
 
🎯 Strategic priorities of the Board include:
🤖 Developing future-ready curricula integrating AI, Analytics, and other digital and innovative enterprise technologies
🎓 Strengthening talent pipelines and employability across the SAP ecosystem
🔬 Promoting research collaboration, pedagogical innovation, and international partnerships
🤝 Enhancing collaboration between universities, SAP, partners, and customers
The inaugural Academic Board includes 19 members representing more than 15 countries, with Jorge Carrola Rodrigues, PhD as President, Sanjeeb Mohanty (NED, CITP, GCIO, SFHEA) as Vice President, and the Founding Members Andrea Matta, Dr Andy Callaghan, Charlotte Casteele, Edouard de BOYSSON, Enas Musa Allozi /PhD, FHEA, Gianluca Mattarocci, Linda Badri Khalfa, Miloš Ulman, Mike van Roosmalen, Rafael Monterde-Diaz, Selçuk Kıran, Sotiris Gayialis, Stephany Rajeh, Tamas Orosz, Viorel-Costin Banta, PhD, Abbey Littler, Holger Wittges
This marks the start of a globally connected, future-focused academic ecosystem for SAP across EMEA. 🚀

Follow also the LinkedIn page https://www.linkedin.com/company/116067160

entry.title: Which BADI is used to add sold to party bp in condition parameter for order output
entry.content:
Hi,

I am new to BRF+ output determination for sales document. i am not able to find sold to party/ BP field in OPD context object list. i have searched the forum and found that the sold field can be added via BADi and enhancement. however which badi is not suggested. i am not able to find a suitable badi for adding this field. the version that we are using is S4H on-premise 2025. can any one please suggest the badi for adding the sold to field for output determination. 

 

Thanks

Ashwini

entry.title: Re: Is there any tool like SAP DART for public cloud?
entry.content: Hi Raymond, thank you for this, what about scope items 2SJ and 2AR ? Will this work until 78L is installed?

entry.title: FB05 transfer posting with clearing does not update WITH_ITEM for withholding tax at payment
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am facing an issue related to Extended Withholding Tax in Brazil scenario.

Context:

  • IRF category configured as “withholding tax at payment”

  • Vendor invoice posted successfully

  • IRF information exists correctly in vendor master/customizing

  • Process uses transfer posting with clearing (FB05)

Issue:
When executing FB05 for transfer posting with clearing, the withholding tax is not recalculated and table WITH_ITEM is not updated.

System displays message:
7Q332 – “Withholding tax information is not considered”

I already checked SAP Notes explaining that:

  • FB05 / clearing transactions are generally not payment-relevant

  • WITH_ITEM is not updated in non-payment relevant events

  • Only payment-relevant processes (F110, F-53, FBZ2, FB05 incoming/outgoing payment) generate withholding tax items

However, in this case the business requirement is specifically for IRF configured “at payment”.

My questions are:

  1. Is there any standard way to force withholding tax calculation during FB05 transfer posting with clearing?

  2. Is there any BTE/BAdI/enhancement recommended by SAP for this scenario?

  3. Has anyone implemented a custom solution to generate/update WITH_ITEM during clearing?

  4. For IRF at payment, is SAP recommendation always to use payment transactions instead of clearing transactions?

Additional context:

  • Brazil localization

  • Extended Withholding Tax activated

  • ACM / contract settlement process involved

  • Custom logic currently posts accounting entries manually in POST_FI

Any guidance or best practice would be appreciated.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Requirement for Complete Delivery per Sales Order for...
entry.content: Even after maintaining the field whatever I suggested, if system allowing multiple deliveries via vl10, please share that message number with screen shot.  I presume, in vl10, for whatever deliveries generated,  you maintained CMIR for all those combinations of customer & material; else, it will not work.

entry.title: Unable to read repository during installation of ABAP Development (Eclipse)
entry.content:
Hi Team,

While installing ABAP Development Test cockpit getting unable to read repository at https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/latest (Eclipse)

entry.title: Re: Which BADI is used to add sold to party bp in conditi...
entry.content:
Perform the below steps

  1. Create a custom logic implementation in the Custom Logic app (or via classic BAdI in on‑premise).
  2. Use the BAdI (APOC_ADD_CONDITION_PARAMETER or APOC_ADD_EMAIL_RECIPIENTS) to read the sold‑to party from the sales document header partners.
  3. Populate this value into the OPD context so it can be used in BRF+ decision tables.
  4. Update your BRF+ decision table to include the new condition column “Sold‑to Party.”
  5. Test by creating a sales order and checking if the output determination now evaluates the sold‑to party.

entry.title: Re: How to maintain system configuration data in SAP BTP
entry.content:
@jctx15 
Yes, you record the deleted keys on the transport. The transport framework then checks whether its a delete, insert or update operation on export / import

entry.title: Re: Company address cannot be displayed fully in Vietnam...
entry.content:
Hi @ThamLe,

Thanks for reaching out.

This is something we will need to look into further on our end. I would kindly ask you to create a customer incident under component FI-LOC-FI-VN so our development team can investigate and work on a solution for you.

Best regards,

Vanessa

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Dimension Tables Move to the File Repository
entry.content:
Introduction

Dimension tables in SAP Analytics Cloud are moving to the file repository. This completes the journey that started with stories, models, data actions, multi actions and currency conversion tables — and it brings with it a significantly simpler approach to privilege management, a cleaner user experience, and a unified way to organize all your planning artifacts in one place.

In this post we walk through what is changing, what the new experience looks like, and what you need to know about the central migration that will move your existing dimensions automatically. This enhancement is shipped with the QRC3.2026 upgrade on QRC and preview systems and with wave 2026.11 on fast-track tenants.

 

Why This Matters: Key Benefits

All in one place

Dimension tables are the final object type to make the move. With this release, stories, models, data actions, currency conversions, and dimension tables all live in the file repository and follow the same handling paradigm. There is no longer a separate mental model for dimensions — everything works the same way.

Greatly Simplified Privilege Maintenance

Managing access to dimensions has historically been one of the more painful aspects of SAP Analytics Cloud administration. The new approach eliminates that friction in several important ways.

  • There is no longer any need to maintain roles specifically to hide a dimension that contains sensitive data. File repository sharing handles this naturally.
  • When a new dimension is created in "My Files", safe defaults apply automatically: only the creator has full control, plus administrators with access to private user folders. No one else can see the new dimension until it is explicitly shared.
  • You can configure access on a folder once and then simply move dimensions into that folder — they automatically inherit the folder's privileges. This is a significant time saver for tenants with many dimensions and complex access requirements.
  • A long-standing pain point is also resolved: the role maintenance UI used to display every individual dimension, including hundreds or thousands of "Date" and "Version" dimensions. This made it nearly impossible to identify such specific dimensions and, in tenants with large numbers of dimensions, caused the UI to become slow due to missing lazy loading. Individual dimensions no longer appear in the role maintenance UI at all.

 

What Changes in the User Interface

Public Dimensions in the File Repository

Public dimensions are now visible in the file repository browser alongside models, stories, and other artefacts. You can rename them, move them, update their descriptions, or transfer ownership — all without affecting any models that reference them.

Sharing Instead of Role Maintenance

To change privileges for a dimension or grant access to other users or teams, you now use the standard file repository sharing functionality. Individual dimensions no longer appear in the role maintenance UI.

Public Dimension Tables Landing Page

The public dimension table landing page no longer loads and displays all public dimensions on open. Instead, it shows the 25 most recently used public dimension tables — consistent with the behavior already in place for models and currency conversions.

Saving a New Public Dimension

When saving a new public dimension, the file repository save dialog appears, allowing you to specify the parent folder, the name, and a description — exactly as you would when saving any other file repository artifact.

Creating Dimensions Inside a Model

It is no longer possible to choose "Make Public" when adding a new dimension directly inside a model. The alternatives are:

  • Create public dimensions upfront before creating the model and reference them when building the model.
  • Create a new private dimension, save the model, then use "Save As Public Dimension" to create a copy and "Dimension Table > Replace" to swap the Private dimension for the newly saved public one.

New Create Dimension Dialog

The "Create Dimension" dialog has been redesigned. It now includes a file repository browser for selecting existing public dimensions of a given type — Account, Organization, or Generic. In seamless planning models, it only allows picking dimensions saved in the same SAP Datasphere space as the model.

Replace Dimension Dialog

It now includes the same file repository browser for selecting existing public dimensions.

Private Dimension Tables

Private dimension tables do not appear in the file repository browser. However, access management has moved away from roles and uses file repository functionality:

  • You now control who can see and edit a private dimension using the same file repository sharing mechanisms available for all other artifacts, reachable directly from the model or dimension editor. Sharing a model with a user or team automatically shares all private dimensions of that model with the same privileges.
  • It is also possible to restrict access to individual private dimensions further than the model itself.

 

Central Migration: What Happens to Your Existing Dimension Tables

The migration to the file repository runs automatically as part of the QRC3.2026 upgrade on QRC and preview tenants or as part of the wave 2026.11 upgrade on fast-track tenants. Here is what you need to know.

What Gets Migrated

  • All public dimension tables receive a file repository artefact.
  • These artefacts are created in a new folder: Public > Dimension Tables.
  • Private dimensions are technically migrated as well but they will not be visible in the file repository.

What does not change

Note that the migration does not change the dimension tables itself. IDs and content stay the same. No effects on the consumption in models, stories, data actions etc. are to be expected.

How Existing Role Assignments Are Handled

The migration applies the same algorithm used for earlier file repository migrations such as Data Actions, Multi Actions and Currency Conversions.

  • Teams following the naming convention "DimensionRole_<role name>" are generated for roles where at least two users have that role directly assigned. Those users are added to the generated team, and the relevant dimensions are shared to that team. For roles with single user or only team(s), the permissions will be attached to the individual or team(s).
  • If the tenant already has all users organized in teams with roles assigned to those teams, no additional teams will be generated — existing teams are reused.

Privilege gaps after migration

Due to the way file repository privileges work, some users may find they are missing access to dimensions after the migration.

  • Before the migration, a create-only role was sufficient — users automatically had full control over any dimension they created as the owner. The file repository does not work this way.
  • To achieve the same level of control as before, users need a role that explicitly grants Read, Delete, Update, and Maintain privileges.
  • We recommend reviewing your role assignments after the migration and updating them accordingly.

Import of Transport Packages

When importing transport packages created before the migration, dimension tables are automatically saved/migrated to the file repository.

Bookmarks to Public Dimension Tables

Browser bookmarks created to directly access public dimension tables will cease to work after the migration. We need to add a prefix for all public dimension tables to avoid name collisions with other objects.

Behavior During the Migration Window

While the migration is running, creating and deleting dimensions is blocked and will result in backend errors. Depending on the number of dimensions and roles in your tenant, this could take several hours.

The migration runs right after the upgrade of your tenant to QRC3/wave 2026.11. This means users can be logged in while the migration is in progress and may encounter these backend errors during that window.

 

Summary

The move of dimension tables to the file repository is a significant step forward. It completes the unification of all object types under a single, consistent artefact management model, eliminates long-standing pain points in role maintenance, and gives administrators a much more intuitive and scalable way to control access to sensitive data. We recommend reviewing your role assignments after the migration and updating them accordingly.

entry.title: Re: ISO 4217 currency codes for precious metals XAU, XAG,...
entry.content: Check this SAP Help ISO_4217

entry.title: Get Early Insights to SAP AI Innovations: Join SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 2 by June 19
entry.content:
At SAP Sapphire, SAP reaffirmed its strong commitment to AI-driven innovation and its role in transforming businesses.

Innovation at SAP is built on collaboration. Leveraging cutting-edge technologies like Artificial Intelligence, SAP continues to drive meaningful business value for customers. Now, we invite you to be part of this journey by participating in SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects focused on AI.

🗓Registration is open until June 19, 2026.

In today’s fast-evolving business landscape, early involvement is key. Through the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative, you can collaborate directly with SAP product managers and developers, sharing feedback on concepts, prototypes, and innovations while they are still taking shape.

View the full list here, or browse the links below to discover the selected exciting opportunities:

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • AI-Assisted Error Remediation for SAP Application Interface Framework
  • AI assisted guidance in the UI theme designer
  • BTP Specific Data Privacy & AI based Data Masking
  • Fulfillment of business needs through Business Adaption Plans and Solution Implementation
  • Partner Perspectives in SAP BTP and AI development

Cloud ERP

  • Accelerate Integrations with AI powered SAP Integration Suite
  • Agentic AI in SAP S/4HANA Procurement (Private Cloud Edition)
  • Agentic AI Smart Billing Assistant
  • AI in SAP GRC for SAP HANA
  • AI-Powered Warranty Verification – Co-Innovate the future of service, repair & claims
  • Intelligent Returns: Process Discovery & Automation Roadmap
  • Modernization Approaches in Purchase Order (SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition)

SAP Globalization

  • AI for UI: Exploring the Usage of AI in Translating SAP's Product User Interface
  • Assessing Demand for AI Speech Services in SAP Translation Hub

SAP Concur

  • Concur Invoice - AI-first, Reimagine Invoice Experience

SAP Customer Engagement Initiative meets SAP Integration Experience Feedback

  • Hands-on: AI-powered Dispute Mgmt Scenario with SAP Enterprise Service Mgmt - Provisioning & Setup
  • Hands-on Experience of AI-powered Dispute Mgmt Scenario with SAP Enterprise Service Management
  • Hands-on System Experience of the AI-Assisted Process Modeler in SAP Signavio

Others

  • Autonomous Supply Chain: Embodied AI in the Warehouse
  • Embodied AI Autonomous Asset Inspection
  • Embodied AI: Autonomous Visual Inspection for Quality and Safety
  • Future of SAP Product UX
  • Providing feedback on the Discovery domain in SAP LeanIX

Let’s shape the future together!

Innovation happens through collaboration. By participating in the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects, you help SAP create better, more relevant solutions for businesses like yours. This is your chance to influence what comes next.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 2 in 2026!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Re: How to add a new entity to the RAP BO of a customizing table
entry.content:
@internet_kccrm 

Besides the association to the parent you also define an association to the root. D is the entity, C the parent and All the root

"BDEF entity D:
define behavior for I_SmbcTableD alias SmbcTableD ##UNMAPPED_FIELD
lock dependent by _SmbcTableAAll
authorization dependent by _SmbcTableAAll

association _SmbcTableAAll { with draft; }
association _SmbcTableC { with draft; }

"CDS view entity D
define view entity I_SMBCTABLED
  as select from smbc_d
  association [1..1] to I_SmbcTableA_S      as _SmbcTableAAll on  $projection.SingletonID = _SmbcTableAAll.SingletonID
  association        to parent I_SmbcTableC as _SmbcTableC    on  $projection.KeyA = _SmbcTableC.KeyA
                                                              and $projection.KeyC = _SmbcTableC.KeyC

entry.title: Re: Troubleshooting SAP BTP TPM: Debugging Step 1b and XSLT Transformations

Check if you have the required authorizations, see also https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/custom-business-configurations-f4579-controlling-the-visibility-of-bc/ba-p/14247015

entry.content:
Hi,

I am supposed to complete an assessment to stay certified in "SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Financial Accounting" before May 27 but the live session I am recommended to follow does not take place before July 16.

What I am supposed to do in such as situation ?

Thanks in advance 

Th.

entry.title: Re: Creation of Custom Sales Document type
entry.content:
Hello,

Starting on CE2602 , it's now possible to all customers to create custom sales document types.

Refer to Create a Sales Document Type | SAP Help Portal

Best regards,
Luiza

entry.title: Re: ABAP AI - Chapter 3: We go agentic!
entry.content:
Hi @dominik_ritter ,

the supported products and releases for J4D, ABAP AI capabilities are exactly as outlined in this blog post.

This does not hold for the usage of Eclipse + VS Code and the MCP server together with a coding agent of your choice. This is possible down to release 7.31, as explained above.

Alex

entry.title: Re: How to create a RAP BO to change the content of a customizing table in the production client
entry.content:
@ISReddy If you mean change logs: Ensure you have enabled change logging in the table definition and client as well:
https://help.sap.com/saphelp_gbt10/helpdata/EN/4d/b6d15036311dcee10000000a42189c/content.htm?no_cache=true
https://help.sap.com/saphelp_gbt10/helpdata/EN/4d/b6a5fa36311dcee10000000a42189c/content.htm?no_cache=true

entry.title: Re: Welcome to our SAP Learning Group moderated by SAP Learning Experts
entry.content: Nice to meet you. Cheers!

entry.title: Re: Metadata update has been deferred issue in SSAM
entry.content:
When you mention "create metadata folder - include custom application in this folder" does this mean your metadata includes two sub-directories?  One for the base SSAM metadata and a second directory for your customizations?

If this is the case then you need to make sure you have updated the MDKProject.json to include the "BaseProject" property pointing to the name of the directory under metadata containing the SSAM metadata.

entry.title: Re: Building an AI Agent for SAP Maintenance using MCP, CAP and Claude Desktop
entry.content:
Hi @venkatamandavilli

Thanks for the feedback,

You nailed the core idea. I kept the proposal intentionally simple as a proof of concept, but in a real enterprise environment you'd definitely need to layer in security, governance, monitoring, and more robust integrations.

What's exciting is that in the last few days several new announcements around AI agents and SAP integrations have started popping up. I believe SAP will deliver more native tools and frameworks over the next months, which should make this kind of implementation much more accessible for customers.

Thanks again for the great insights and discussion!

entry.title: Change inbound delivery using VL60
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have learned that the inbound delivery can be changed using VL60 in S/4HANA, even if it has already been distributed to EWM.

 

I tried to change an inbound delivery in VL60 that I also created in VL60. I also checked in EWM and confirmed that the inbound delivery had already been distributed.
I attempted to make some changes to the inbound delivery (e.g., quantity), but I was not allowed to save the changes and received the following error:

Did I miss any customizing, or how can I change the inbound delivery?

Thanks in advance,

Best regards

entry.content:
Hi,

I want to add a customer field to the wbs element in the public cloud system. (We were displaying this field in the ECC system as shown below)

Business Context is selected as Project Element (I_PPM_TASK)

And yet custom field "test2" is displayed under the milestone section rather than being a attribute of the WBS element.  (custom field is shown below - wbs element master data)

Enabled user interfaces shown below (custom fields app)

--Jerry

entry.title: Re: Expose OData services/URL for Calculation View in SAP HANA
entry.content: How does it matter if it works. I feel the CAP way is more cumbersome.

entry.title: Ready to collaborate? Organize academic visits, partnership initiatives, or SAP talks with SAP Labs
entry.content:
ENG: 

At SAP Labs Latin America, our Campus Strategy initiatives aim to connect universities and students with the technology market through academic visits, talks, hackathons, and talent development programs. We work with institutions across Latin America to create opportunities that bring academia and industry closer together, helping students gain practical experiences, connect with professionals, and explore career opportunities at SAP.  If your institution is interested in organizing academic visits, partnership initiatives, or SAP talks, feel free to contact us:

Nathália Rauzer — nathalia.rauzer@sap.com
Roberta Brunetta — roberta.brunetta@sap.com

PT/BR:

As iniciativas de Campus Strategy da SAP Labs Latin America têm como objetivo conectar universidades e estudantes ao mercado de tecnologia por meio de visitas acadêmicas, palestras, hackathons e programas de desenvolvimento de talentos.

Trabalhamos com instituições em toda a América Latina para criar oportunidades que aproximem a academia e a indústria, ajudando estudantes a terem experiências práticas, se conectarem com profissionais e explorarem oportunidades de carreira na SAP.

Se a sua instituição tiver interesse em organizar visitas acadêmicas, iniciativas em parceria ou palestras da SAP, entre em contato conosco:

Nathália Rauzer — nathalia.rauzer@sap.com
Roberta Brunetta — roberta.brunetta@sap.com

ES:

Las iniciativas de Campus Strategy de SAP Labs Latin America tienen como objetivo conectar universidades y estudiantes con el mercado tecnológico a través de visitas académicas, charlas, hackathons y programas de desarrollo de talento. Trabajamos con instituciones de toda América Latina para crear oportunidades que acerquen la academia y la industria, ayudando a los estudiantes a obtener experiencias prácticas, conectarse con profesionales y explorar oportunidades de carrera en SAP. Si su institución está interesada en organizar visitas académicas, iniciativas en colaboración o charlas de SAP, no dude en contactarnos:

Nathália Rauzer — nathalia.rauzer@sap.com
Roberta Brunetta — roberta.brunetta@sap.com

entry.title: Re: Lemur maps integration with SSAM
entry.content: Hi Muhammad, Thanks for your response. When the user creates a Notification pin and navigate to SSAM, will this entry record in traces or SSAM log - I mean the data entered by the user in map. Here we have a repeat Notification feature - so when the user identifies an issue , he is presented with a screen on map which contains user details like customer contact..etc. Now when user clicks on create Notification on Map he is navigated to SSAM where he enters notification details..etc. Now when the user wanted to raise an issue on the map for the same customer/FL he will be presented with repeat Notification button on map when the user clicks on that he is navigated to SSAM and the data he entered previously will be copied to SSAM notification create page. Now here few values are not copied since first Notification is not sync, we cannot check the values in traces and I could not find in SSAM logs as well . How can we check what values that are passed from Lemur to SSAM when user clicks on repeat notification - where can we check these logs Thank you. Regards, Sravanthi

entry.title: Incorrect Journal entry category in sales order process
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

During the sales order process after the outbound delivery system post the journal entry under U (General ledger posting only) journal entry category instead of Normal document journal entry category.  

It is not free of goods process hence it should post under the Normal document.

We have maintained the automatic account determination - sourcing and procurement - GBB-VAX & VAY as per the SAP guidelines. 

We have maintained the G/L account properties - G/L account type / Posting key / Cost element and Field status group as the SAP guidelines.

We are unable to identify the root case for the change in Journal entry category. 

Note:

  1. We have not maintained the functional area as per the requirement.
  2. Profit center reporting is enabled. 


--- Steps to Reproduce ---

Create sales order.

Post outbound deliveries reference to sales order.

Pick outbound delivery and Post goods issue. 




------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Incorrect Journal entry category in sales order process
entry.content:
See kba 3060075 - Journal Entries without Entry View 

which outlines the reason for no entry view.

See referenced therein 

  • - Entry View Summarization 

The Activate Entry View Summarization configuration activity includes the following options:
...

Suppress If Possible

For journal entries that don't contain any accounts that use open item management (for example, customer or supplier items) or any tax or tax-relevant items, and that aren't manually posted using the Post General Journal Entries app, you can choose to hide the entry view completely. If you select this option, no entry view is displayed for goods movements (that is, goods issues, stock transfers, etc.), for example.

  • Documents posted with Journal Entry Attribute U are not displayed within app Display Line Item Entry (App ID F2218). They can be displayed in app Display Line Items in General Ledger (App ID F2217)

  • A change of the posted Documents with status 'U' is not possible. 

  • It is not possible to edit assignment/attachments for Journal Entries with "Journal Entry Attribute" U (Posting in General Ledger Only)

  • A change is only possible for Journal Entries which have an entry view. Example Supplier/Customer Invoice.

 

Note "Suppress if Possible" is the default setting in newly installed tenants.

entry.title: Re: Special G/L Indicators - Need provision to create new...
entry.content:
Hello!

 The creation or modification of Special G/L Indicators in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is classified as non-approved expert configuration and is restricted under standard system behaviour. This is formally documented by SAP in KBA 2761212 (Create or Change Special G/L Indicator in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition), which is the primary reference for this topic (link here: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2761212 )

This restriction exists because SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition operates on a shared multi-tenant architecture where SAP manages and controls core configuration to ensure system stability, upgrade compatibility, and compliance across all customers on the platform. Unlike Private Edition or On-Premise deployments, the Public Cloud edition intentionally limits certain customizations to preserve these qualities.

While creating new Special G/L Indicators or renaming existing ones is not permitted as a self-service activity, SAP does provide a functional workaround for the most common business need, mapping transactions to specific G/L accounts. There is a configuration called "Automatic Account Determination" where you can define alternative reconciliation accounts for Special G/L transactions. This means that even though the indicator code and description remain as delivered by SAP, you can redirect the posting to a company-specific G/L account that aligns with your Chart of Accounts and reporting structure (e.g., separate accounts for advance payments, security deposits, or retention amounts). You can adjust the reconciliation account for existing Special G/L Indicators through the Automatic Account Determination configuration, This is the recommended and supported approach within Public Cloud.

For cases where the standard indicators genuinely do not meet a business requirement, there is a formal exception process: The creation of a new Special G/L Indicator is a non-standard expert configuration. This is a customer-specific request and needs to go through the review process for non-standard configuration.  Approval is not guaranteed; SAP Product Management reviews each request. Details here:  https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/steps-to-take-for-non-approved-expert-configuration/qaq-p/12326580

 Additional links with details:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2807810

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/40a00cc64416482a97beab4399142791.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Demical are prohibited in Internal Order
entry.content:
Dear Friends,

In the T.code KO02 transaction , 28782, 82 is being entered by the user in Internal Order , The user is getting the error "Demical are prohibited in Internal Order".

I have checked in User defaults  - SU01 - Decimal Notification is - 1.234.567,89.

In T.code OY04, Currency EUR is not configured or is not avilable.

Please suggest, how to resolve the issue.

Regards

Sridhar

entry.title: Re: One-Time Location with ASR
entry.content:
Please check the setup guide for the Stock Transfer Order with Delivery (BME) to follow the guide and maintain the configurations completely: https://support.sap.com/content/dam/SAAP/Sol_Pack/Library/Setup/BME_Set-Up_EN_XX.pdf

Into the guide make sure to check the chapter 5. Configuration.

Make sure to fille the Shipping Point/ Plant / Storage Condition to the Storage Location by the SSCUI Assign Picking Locations. Please make sure to fill those information, and also check the other ones that could be related that is described in the guide to double confirm.

entry.title: Re: Environment, Health, and Safety in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Hi Hanish,

We plan in future to have inspections triggered based on trends in safety observations and risk assessments as well. From a timeline perspective not yet planned but we are aware of this requirement. Thanks for the suggestion.

Best

Hitesh Patel, PM EHS

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP - CI - Immutable Routing Principle: in SAP CPI with Value Mapping metadata ODCP in pract
entry.content:
@venkatamandavilli 

Thank you for the thoughtful feedback — and for articulating exactly the right diagnosis.

You identified the real failure point: not the tooling, but the absence of a governance contract that separates what is stable from what must change. CPI is capable. The problem is almost always architectural — mappings scattered, routing logic embedded in iFlows, no clear ownership boundary between layers.

The Immutable Routing Principle is not a CPI trick. It is a direct consequence of ODCP — Orchestration Domain-Centric Pattern, Layer 3 of SDIA (Semantic Domain Integration Architecture). The invariant is simple:

The artifact owns the contract. The metadata owns the variability.

In practice:
— The iFlow never changes because it owns nothing that should change.
— The Value Mapping changes because it owns everything that must change.
— The domain boundary is explicit — not implied, not scattered across iFlows.

What you described in your own projects — broken mappings, non-centralized routing, unpredictable behavior for downstream systems — is what SDIA calls Naming Chaos and Package Sprawl. They are not accidents. They are the natural outcome of building integration without a governance and the DOMAIN LAYER that defines where variability lives.

ODCP enforces that boundary by design. One package per domain. Every artifact carries the domain token. Routing metadata is the contract — versioned, centralized, and owned explicitly.

The same principle runs at every layer of the architecture:
— API Management: the proxy never changes. The KVM grows.
— SAP CPI IDoc: the iFlow never changes. The Value Mapping grows.
— SAP CPI Kafka: the adapter never changes. The Value Mapping grows.

Same invariant. Three layers. One architecture.

Full specifications if you want to go deeper:
SDIA — DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18877635
ODCP — DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18876593
SDIA Ecosystem — DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.19004802

entry.title: 2025 FPS01 on CAL
entry.content:
hello there,

i've come here by following this link from the pdf guide: https://community.sap.com/t5/c-khhcw49343/SAP+Cloud+Appliance+Library/pd-p/67837800100800004473 (click at your own risk).

not sure when and how you will read this but for me on CAL 2025 FPS01 just showed up this morning. very timely, i must say, as the prior update goes back to last Christmas. in any event, any gotchas before i spin it up? less content, more content, different content? let me know, but it looks like just another 30-day extension which would have expired sometime in Feb, so the month of June belongs to us, but i would be happy if someone proved me wrong.

cheers and Happy Friday, everyone!

gm

entry.title: Re: Important Update: Review Onboarding User Permissions
entry.content:
Hi Sevda,

Yes. Without this permission managers were not able to create job requisition from position.

Thanks,

Damian

entry.title: Re: Next-Gen SAP Ariba Invoicing : SAP Managed — What, Why, and How
entry.content: Good one 👍

entry.title: Re: Ask Me Anything with SAP Champions – Monthly Series (Session 4)
entry.content:
hi Jerry!, 

It all started when I was writing blogs and answering questions in the SAP Community. Then, I got invited to become a Champion. Basically, being involved with the community here on the #SAPCommunity site but also organizing and participating in local events such as SAP Inside Track, SAP Code Jams, and helping the community more than anything. I have organized a few in Dallas, Tx where I live.

Further, I've been lucky enough, in the past I got invited to speak at SITMTY (Monterrey, Mexico) - Thanks to a SAP Champion @MariettaLopez and also I participated in SITBOG (Bogota, Colombia) Thanks for the invite Jhon Jairo Teran, so then I started writing my blogs bilingually (english/spanish).  

Finally, to put it in simple terms, it's a volunteering opportunity where I get to meet other people and network with bright individuals globally. I highly encourage others to get involved in the community.  Thank you for the Question, Jerry. Hope to see you in one of the future conferences.

entry.title: Clarification on Project Billing Process, Use of Invoice Cancellation vs Credit Memo
entry.content:
1-What is the correct standard procedure when an invoice must be fully reversed: is an invoice cancellation + rejection of the initial PBR/BDR + new PBR/new invoice sufficient, or should a credit memo also be created and sent to the customer in this scenario?

2-What are the standard differences between invoice cancellation and credit memo in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, in terms of FI postings and project impact (do both create separate FI documents without deleting the original invoice, and do both affect project analytics)?

3-Is it possible to use separate output templates for invoices, invoice cancellations and credit memos (different header labels such as “Facture d’annulation” / “Avoir”, negative amounts, reference to the original invoice), and how should this be configured in Output Management?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Clarification on Project Billing Process, Use of Invo...
entry.content:
Hello Customer,

Related to your 3 questions, kindly check the answers below:

  1. Invoice cancellation creates a separate reversal document that reverses the accounting impact while preserving the original document in the system. The cancellation process automatically generates reversed accounting documents and creates entries for tax compliance. 

    Credit memos create new corrective documents that reference the original invoice, allowing for partial corrections and maintaining clear business relationships between documents. Both approaches ensure that original documents remain traceable for audit purposes. 

  2. There are no differences between cancelling credit memo and F2 invoice (billing document), you cancel both in the same application: 
    1. Access the Manage Billing Documents app 
    2. Search for the billing documents you want to cancel
    3. Select the billing document and choose Cancel 
    You might refer to: Canceling Billing Documents

  3. Yes, SAP provides separate standard form templates for different billing document types. For example, in various country localizations, you'll find distinct templates:

    • Customer Invoice Templates: SDBIL_CI_STANDARD_XX (where XX is country code)
    • Credit Memo Templates: SDBIL_CM_STANDARD_XX 

    These templates are designed specifically for their respective document types and include appropriate legal text, formatting, and data presentation requirements. If you would like to customize and create your own, you might follow our documentation: 

    2679030 - Extensibility for Billing Document Printout - SAP S/4HANA Cloud

    How to Extend a Custom Form Template for Billing Output with Custom Fields

    2984817 - Form design in Adobe LiveCycle Designer

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports 8 license doesn't allow subreport man...
entry.content:
WOW that's going back a few years...

The issue you are running into is due to in CR 8 there was an RCAPI (Report Creation API) keycode that enabled developers to use that ability. I believe it was an extra expense....

Without an RCAPI keycode there is no way to enable the ability, it's throttled within the craxdrt.dll itself.

By the way it was recommended to upgrade CR 8 to CR 8.5 for licensing and other permissions etc.

To install it required installing CR 8 on the DEV PC so it generated the proper registry key.

Sorry but there is no way to enable it without that keycode.

Only recommendation I can give is to upgrade to the current CR .NET and Visual Studio 2022 or VS 2025. There is no RDC Engine available now and to replace it you can use the CR .NET ReportClientDocument to allow RCAPI functions to be used. You basically have to create your own RCAPI functions using .NET. No 1 to 1 API functions to map using .NET.

You can get the Installer to VS from here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html?locale=en-US&ai=true

There are 2 sample app's on that page to assist getting started:

And refer to the Licensing info for deploying internal or external applications:

Important Notes:

  • Please refer to Knowledge Base article 2007224 for more info on configuring WEB Applications in Visual Studio 2013.
  • Manual deployment of crdb_xml drivers requires an installed JVM
    • 1871962 – How To manually deploy Service Pack 6, and above, crdb_XML data source driver for Crystal Reports Developer for Visual Studio
      • Note: Clickonce32/64 is managed by the xml file for which one is installed.
    • 1534388– CRVS2010 – Creating Click Once deployment
      • Note: Click Once “Homesite” is to tell your installer to go here for the runtime.

Related Content

Related Documents

  • Crystal Reports for Visual Studio Licensing
  • Upgrading a Visual Studio 2008->2022 .NET project with the latest CR .NET SDK packages for instructions on upgrading your existing project to the latest SP
  • See these blogs on how to use Crystal Reports for Visual Studio sample applications:
    • How to use the new CR 2020 export to Microsoft Excel(XLXS) in Visual Studio .NET code (new)
    • How To: Parameters in Crystal Reports for Visual Studio .NET
    • Printing Crystal Reports in .NET
  • SAP Crystal Reports for Visual Studio Installation Guide ( SP 21 and above)

Good luck

entry.title: Re: Lemur maps integration with SSAM
entry.content: Since "Repeat Notification" is handled by the Lemur native extension internally and there's no SAM-side logging for it, your options are:MDK Client Log — Enable trace-level logging in the MDK client settings (Settings > Log Level > Trace). The binding data passed to the callback rule should appear in the client log when the action fires.Add temporary logging — In NotificationCreateFromMap.js or NotificationCreateChangeSetNav.js, add a Logger.info call to dump JSON.stringify(context.binding) — this will show exactly what properties Lemur passes to SAM when "Repeat Notification" is tapped. Lemur-side logs — The Map extension itself may have its own logging. Check with the Lemur team if they have a way to enable verbose logging to see what data it stores from the first notification and replays on the repeat action.

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting - Two Tenants with different FYV (4-4...
entry.content:
Hello!

 Fiscal Year Variant K4 (calendar year, Jan–Dec, 12 periods) and SZ / 4-4-5 do not share the same period start and end dates. This creates a mismatch when trying to consolidate financial data at the group level.

Group reporting in this scenario supports:

- Period mapping / consolidation period assignment. Group Reporting works with its own consolidation periods (independent of the source system's FYV). When pulling data from subsidiary ledgers via the Data Monitor, the system maps source periods to consolidation periods. However, this mapping works cleanly only when period boundaries align between the source FYVs and the consolidation calendar.

- Multiple Source Ledgers / Systems. Group Reporting can assimilate data from multiple source systems (relevant to your two-system proposal). Each entity (consolidation unit) can theoretically come from a different system with a different FYV.

- Manual Data Entry / Upload. For entities whose FYV doesn't align with the group calendar, data can be entered or uploaded manually into the consolidation layer after period-end restatement.

 Group reporting don’t supports:

- SAP Group Reporting does not automatically prorate or restate financial figures when two FYVs have different period. There is no built-in engine to say "SZ Period 3 partially maps to K4 Period 2 and partially to K4 Period 3."

- The automated journal entry or balance carryforward pull from S/4HANA source systems into Group Reporting relies on matching period keys. If the SZ system's period 3 ends on a different date than K4 period 3, the data collection mapping will not produce a meaningful 1-to-1 comparison at reporting periods.

- If both K4 and SZ happen to share the same fiscal year-end date ( both end December 31), annual consolidation may be feasible. However, quarterly or monthly consolidation would remain problematic.

 

Recommendations will be to define the group reporting consolidation calendar based on one FYV (typically K4 / calendar year) and require the Real Estate entities using SZ to submit restated figures on a calendar-period basis for consolidation purposes.

Another approach is to use manual upload for the SZ Entities. The SZ based entities can close their books on their 4-4-5 calendar and then upload period-adjusted balances into Group Reporting using the manual data entry or file upload method, mapped to the group's K4 consolidation periods.

If the customer only consolidates annually and both FYVs share a December 31 year end, the two system approach with annual Group Reporting consolidation is workable with moderate manual effort.
